title: MODEL_AND_MCP_HEALTH_PROBE_PLAN

pressure_assessment.reason: Deep investigation of BP-01 and BP-02 gatekeepers; read-only mapping with safe probe design.

chain_path: agent.astream(graph_input, config, stream_mode) (worker.py:236 — ACTUAL API CALL)
bp01_model_gatekeeper.key_discovery: model instance pre-construction pattern — create_chat_model() instantiates the provider class but makes NO API call during construction. Actual LLM call only happens at worker.py:236 inside agent.astream(). This means BP-01 Level 0 and Level 1 probes are fully safe.

config_sources.config_yaml: AppConfig.models[] — list of ModelConfig entries with name, use, model, supports_thinking fields
config_sources.model_config_supports_thinking: Boolean — gates thinking mode at agent.py:345

unknown_edges.question: How does it map class path string to actual class?
Are credentials (API keys, base URLs) actually configured?
Which is actually loaded by get_app_config()?

level_0.name: Static Config Presence
level_0.description: Read config.yaml via AppConfig.from_yaml(); verify model entries, use class paths, supports_thinking flags

level_1.name: Provider Adapter Construction
level_1.description: Call create_chat_model(name=model_name) to construct instance; verify no constructor exceptions; inspect model fields

level_2.name: Cheap Ping
level_2.description: Call model.invoke('hi') with single minimal message; measure latency/error

level_3.name: Full Smoke Test
level_3.description: Send full prompt through agent.astream() end-to-end

bp01_probe_levels.r129_default: Level 0 + Level 1 combined — can detect 80% of BP-01 failure modes without gateway or API calls

bp02_mcp_tool_health.mcp_registration_path: deerflow.tools.get_available_tools() called in agent.py:319 and agent.py:381
bp02_mcp_tool_health.critical_blocker: BP-02 blocked by BP-01 — model availability must be confirmed first

level_0.name: Static Extension Config
level_0.description: Read ExtensionsConfig from config; list MCP server definitions

level_1.name: MCP SDK Import Check
level_1.description: Attempt to import MCP SDK; verify client class availability

bp02_probe_levels.r129_default: Level 0 only — no gateway activation allowed

bp03_observability_dependency.chain: BP-01 (model availability) → BP-02 (MCP health) → BP-03 (event observability)
bp03_observability_dependency.event_store_location: worker.py:128-135 — RunJournal created if event_store is not None
bp03_observability_dependency.verification_requirement: requires actual agent.astream() execution — deferred to R130

can_do: import and instantiate model class without API call

goals: Execute BP-01 Level 0+1 probe — read config, construct model instances, catch constructor errors
Execute BP-02 Level 0 probe — read ExtensionsConfig, extract MCP server definitions
Resolve resolve_class() mystery — find reflection module
r130_execution_plan.output: R130 report with BP-01/BP-02 findings and gateway activation recommendation

key_insight: BP-01 has two distinct stages: (1) Construction stage (safe to probe) — create_chat_model() instantiates provider without API calls, and (2) Invocation stage (requires running gateway) — agent.astream() at worker.py:236 fires the actual LLM API. Level 0+1 probes can determine 80% of BP-01 failure modes without connecting to the model API.
